what_it_does: Implements a browser-local project context store attached to window.MS_CONTEXT, persisted to localStorage per project id, with helpers for managing the brief, sources, connector state, and an event log, plus a React hook that subscribes to store updates.
observations: State is an object with brief, sources, connectors, log, and updatedAt fields.
Persistence uses a localStorage key prefix 'ms_ctx_' plus the current projectId.
Subscribers are stored in a Set and notified on load and persist.
Sources receive generated ids, a timestamp, and default willRead=true.
Blob object URLs are stripped from sources before serialization.
The event log is capped at the most recent 200 entries.
Errors during load, parse, and persist are silently swallowed.
useContextStore spreads the state object to trigger React re-renders.
standouts: localStorage persistence with explicit blob-URL stripping.
Built-in pub/sub subscription model inside an IIFE.
A running event log is maintained alongside project data.
conventions: IIFE module attached to a window global.
Direct mutation of shared state followed by an explicit persist call.
React useState/useEffect hook for store subscription.
concerns: Direct mutation of shared state can make change tracking fragile.
No schema version or migration for persisted data.
localStorage quota errors are not handled.
useContextStore returns the entire state, which may cause frequent re-renders.
Source ids use Math.random, which is not collision-safe at scale.
notes: This is the in-browser project memory layer: it records the brief, sources, connectors, and a short log, persisting everything to localStorage by project id.
questions: How is the initial projectId chosen or changed beyond calling load?
Is there a migration strategy if the persisted state shape changes?
Where is the actual binary or text content of sources stored?
How are connector tokens refreshed or revoked?
Is useContextStore intended to be used by many components or a single provider?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it records and persists project context information that the agent reads from and writes to.
intention_signals: Provide a lightweight, always-available project context ledger that survives page reloads and can be observed by React components.
novelty: The combination of a handoff-style 'project carries its own context' ledger with blob-URL-aware persistence and a built-in event log.
contribution: A concrete, file-local implementation of a persisted project context model with subscription-based reactivity, giving the system a place to store and retrieve briefs, sources, connector status, and history without external dependencies.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-local project context store that persists briefs, sources, connectors, and an event log to localStorage and exposes a pub/sub API plus a React hook.
intention_for_embedding: To give the Counterpart project a durable, observable memory of intake data and ongoing activity that survives reloads.

symbols.does: Returns a fresh default state object.
symbols.description: Creates the initial context state with an empty brief, sources array, connectors object, log array, and a current updatedAt timestamp.

symbols.does: Loads persisted state for a project id.
symbols.description: Sets the active projectId, reads and parses JSON from localStorage under KEY+projectId, falls back to a blank state, notifies subscribers, and returns the loaded state.

symbols.does: Serializes state to localStorage and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Updates updatedAt, strips blob: URLs from source metadata, saves a JSON snapshot to localStorage, and calls notify.

symbols.does: Calls all subscriber callbacks with current state.
symbols.description: Iterates the subscriber Set, invoking each callback with the current state and swallowing any thrown errors.

symbols.does: Registers a callback and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: Adds the provided function to the internal subscribers Set and returns a function that removes it.
Exposes the internal state object directly to callers.
Assigns the provided brief object to state.brief and triggers persist.

symbols.does: Adds a source item and logs the addition.
symbols.description: Creates a source item with a generated id, timestamp, and willRead=true, pushes it to state.sources, logs the addition, and persists.
Filters state.sources to exclude the item with the matching id and persists the result.

symbols.does: Toggles whether a source will be read.
symbols.description: Finds the source by id, sets its willRead flag, and persists.

symbols.does: Updates connector state and logs the change.
symbols.description: Merges the provided data into state.connectors[providerId], logs the status, and persists.

symbols.does: Appends a timestamped event to the log.
symbols.description: Pushes a {t, kind, text} entry onto state.log and truncates the log to the most recent 200 entries.
